message:
fix(fr-store): function_load meta-header validation order matches upstream (br-frankenredis-r85v)

Self-review caught a sixth real defect in the FUNCTION load path:
the new validation gates fired in the wrong order. A script with
both a bad engine and a bad library name surfaced the name-charset
error first, but upstream functions.c validates in the order
engine-present -> engine-registered -> name-present -> name-charset.
Conformance fixtures and downstream peers compare error wording
exactly; precedence drift can mask the engine bug while showing
a name bug, leading to confusing diagnostics.

Reorder the four gates to match upstream:
  1. engine empty -> 'Missing library metadata'
  2. engine != LUA -> 'Engine 'X' not found'
  3. name empty -> 'Library name was not given'
  4. name charset invalid -> 'Library names can only contain ...'

New test function_load_meta_header_validation_order_matches_upstream
walks all four gate boundaries and confirms each surfaces in the
upstream-expected position.

fr-store 295 passed; cargo clippy --tests -D warnings clean;
cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r85v.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
